On a fresh box there is no config, so residents()->taste_readiness()->ensure_config() raises 'earcrate is not configured yet' (app.py:970-972). The 'Book a set' button (index.html:249) has NO config guard; clicking it runs one_click_taste_mix -> ensure_config() raises -> caught into status.last_error (app.py:5225-5227) -> user sees red 'not configured' on Activity. configure() WRITES the workspace pointer to visible_app_dir() (app.py:539) but load_config_if_present() READS by scanning pointer_search_dirs() (app.py:448-449), which depends on how the process was started (util.py:90-106). When they disagree, setup succeeds but the next launch via a different entry point cannot find the pointer -> config stays None -> all downstream throws 'not configured'. MILESTONES.md:46-47 records this exact defect.

_valid_pointer() returns None if master_root does not exist on disk (app.py:470-471). A correctly-persisted config silently fails to load if the music drive is unmounted or the folder renamed; UI then lies 'not configured' instead of 'your music folder moved'.

qa_findings.title: Identify and Deep-clean wedge the engine "busy" forever (never reset on success)
qa_findings.where: earcrate/app.py:5217-5229 (run_background) + identify_tracks (3946-3984) / apply_identities (3993-4089) / deep_clean_scan (3794-3840); routes server.py:311-318
qa_findings.impact: /api/identify, /api/identify/apply and /api/deepclean/scan are dispatched through run_background, which sets status busy=True at start and only resets busy=False inside the exception handler. On SUCCESS it never resets busy. Unlike scan/analyze/one_click/manifest (which call set_status(..., busy=False) at their own end — see 1604, 1802, 3420, 4305), identify_tracks/apply_identities/deep_clean_scan never call set_status at all. So after clicking 'Identify (opt-in)' or Deep-clean 'Scan', the engine is stuck showing ENGINE BUSY permanently. Worse, every subsequent run_background call then raises RuntimeError('EarCrate is already busy') (5219-5220), which the POST handler turns into a 500 (server.py:340-348) that libAction's empty catch(e){} swallows silently. Because the only code that clears busy is other run_background jobs (themselves rejected), the whole app is wedged until a server restart.
qa_findings.fix: Have run_background reset busy in a finally/success path (e.g. self.set_status('done', 1, False) after fn() returns unless the fn already set it), or make identify_tracks/apply_identities/deep_clean_scan call set_status(busy=False) on completion. Ideally these read-only assessments should not be backgrounded at all since callers want their return payload.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. run_background (app.py:5221) sets busy=True and only resets busy=False in its except handler (5227); on success (5224 discards fn's return, 5228 returns started) nothing clears busy. deep_clean_scan (3794-3840), identify_tracks (3946-3984) and apply_identities (3993-4089) contain NO set_status call at all (grep of set_status confirms none in that range), unlike scan/analyze/one_click/manifest which each call set_status(...,busy=False) at 1604/1802/3420/4305. So after a successful /api/identify, /api/identify/apply or /api/deepclean/scan the engine stays busy=True permanently, and every later run_background job raises RuntimeError('already busy') (5219-5220) -> POST 500 (server.py:340-348). One correction to the tester's wording: the 500 is NOT fully silent -- post() (index.html:157) toasts '✕ EarCrate is already busy' before libAction's empty catch (546) swallows the rethrow. The permanent wedge until server restart is real; blocker stands.

qa_findings.title: Workbench "Render ▸" throws away the composed/loaded plan and renders a different, freshly-composed set
qa_findings.impact: The entire Workbench pipeline (Propose plan → edit → Save/Load plan) culminates in the primary "Render ▸" button, and the rail visualization shows window.__plan as the thing about to be rendered. But renderMix() posts only {taste_profile:S.resident} to /api/one_click_bg, which runs the full cold-start one_click_taste_mix. That calls propose_taste_mashup, which internally calls compose_taste_arrangement with a brand-new seed (next_render_seed, app.py:3020) and composes a completely fresh arrangement from the approved pool — window.__plan is never sent and never read. The WAV the user gets is NOT the plan they proposed, tuned, saved, or loaded. Preflight/Save/Load are effectively decorative because Render ignores their output. A plan-execute path exists (/api/manifest/execute_bg, produced by propose_taste_mashup['manifest']) but Render does not use it.
qa_findings.fix: Make renderMix render the current plan: if window.__plan exists, POST its manifest to /api/manifest/execute_bg (or add a plan-aware render endpoint that takes the arrangement/plan_hash), instead of re-running one_click_taste_mix. Alternatively relabel the button so it doesn't imply it renders the displayed plan.
qa_findings.verify_note: renderMix (index.html:429) posts only {taste_profile:S.resident} to /api/one_click_bg. server.py:301-302 routes that to run_background(one_click_mix,...) -> one_click_taste_mix (app.py:3300), which runs the full cold-start pipeline and calls propose_taste_mashup (app.py:3387). propose_taste_mashup composes a brand-new arrangement from the approved pool with a fresh seed via next_render_seed (app.py:3020/3023). window.__plan is never in the POST body and never read anywhere server-side. The finding is actually stronger than stated: the Workbench propose route (propose_plan, app.py:4977-4998) explicitly composes WITHOUT writing a manifest (docstring line 4978-4981) and returns only {arrangement,score,taste_gate,seed} -- so window.__plan carries no manifest and there is genuinely no wired path to execute the displayed plan (the /api/manifest/execute_bg route the tester references exists but is never fed the propose output). Propose/Save/Load are decorative w.r.t. Render. Confirmed high.

qa_findings.title: "Migrate legacy" button only ever runs the dry-run plan — there is no way to apply a migration from the UI
qa_findings.impact: The only migration control in the UI calls /api/migrate/plan, which returns dry_run:true and touches nothing (app.py:892-915). /api/migrate/apply is never called anywhere in index.html (grep confirms it appears only in server.py and CHANGELOG). Because apply_workspace_migration requires the plan's signature (app.py:920-927), and the frontend never captures or resubmits that signature, the entire SIMULATE->APPROVE->APPLY workflow is unreachable. A user who clicks "Migrate legacy" can preview forever but can never actually migrate.
qa_findings.fix: Add an Apply control that resubmits the signature from the plan response to /api/migrate/apply (mirroring how reorgApply reuses REORG.signature at index.html:520), or relabel the button to make clear it is preview-only.

qa_findings.title: /api/rollback (rollback_outputs) endpoint has no frontend caller — output rollback is unreachable and no manifest_id is ever passed
qa_findings.where: server.py:335-336; no reference to '/api/rollback' anywhere in index.html
qa_findings.impact: The Ingest card advertises output as "manifest-journaled, rollback-able" (index.html:483), but grep for '/api/rollback' and 'manifest_id' in the frontend finds zero call sites. rollback_outputs (app.py:4308) is only invocable via raw API/CLI. The task's concern that rollback "targets a real manifest_id" cannot even be satisfied from the UI because the UI never sends one. Note the backend treats empty manifest_id as "roll back every manifest's archive_moves" (app.py:4321 skips the filter when manifest_id is falsy), so any future UI wiring that forgets to pass a manifest_id would silently roll back ALL outputs.
qa_findings.fix: Either add a rollback control that lists manifests and posts a specific manifest_id (with apply confirmation), or drop the "rollback-able" claim. If a UI is added, require a non-empty manifest_id so an empty value can't trigger a global rollback.

qa_findings.title: 500 traceback returned by the backend is fetched but never shown anywhere in the UI
qa_findings.impact: The backend deliberately computes and returns {error, trace} on every 500 (server.py:236 GET, server.py:348 POST) and even writes the full traceback to DEBUG_LOG. But api() reads only `j.error` (index.html:155: `throw new Error(j.error||txt)`), and the string 'trace' appears NOWHERE in index.html. The traceback is completely discarded. The best a user ever sees is a single-line str(exc) inside a 6-second toast (only for POST calls). There is no panel, no console surfacing, no persistent error view. This is a central reason the app 'borks silently': the diagnostic the backend went out of its way to send is thrown away by the client.
qa_findings.fix: In api(), when !r.ok and j.trace exists, surface it — e.g. log to console (`console.error(j.trace)`) and/or render it into a dismissable global error panel. At minimum include j.trace in the thrown Error so a caller can display it. Do not rely solely on a 6s toast for a stack trace.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. server.py:236 (GET) and server.py:348 (POST) both return {"error": str(exc), "trace": traceback.format_exc()} on 500. In index.html, api() at line 155 reads only j.error: `if(!r.ok) throw new Error(j.error||txt)`. A grep for 'trace' in index.html returns ZERO hits, and there are NO console.* calls anywhere in the file — so j.trace is silently discarded and never logged. post() (line 157) only surfaces e.message via a 6s toast, and that only fires for POST calls; GET failures go through empty `catch(e){}` blocks (see renderSessions:434, renderCrate:289) and show nothing at all. The diagnostic the backend deliberately builds is thrown away by the client. Fix as described (include j.trace in the thrown Error and/or console.error it).

qa_findings.title: pollStatus swallows all errors while the status dot defaults to green 'SYSTEM ONLINE' — the heartbeat lies on failure
qa_findings.impact: pollStatus wraps everything in `try{...}catch(e){}` (index.html:609) with an empty catch. The status bar's initial markup hard-codes a green accent dot and the text 'SYSTEM ONLINE' (index.html:60). If /api/status returns 403 (bad/missing token) or 500, the poll fails silently every 2s (index.html:618) and onlineDot/onlineTxt/engineTxt are never updated — so the UI keeps displaying a green 'SYSTEM ONLINE' dot forever even though every request is failing. The single liveness indicator actively reports the opposite of the truth. This is the core silent-bork: the user has no signal that the server/engine is unreachable or the token is wrong.
qa_findings.fix: In pollStatus's catch, set onlineDot to a fault color and onlineTxt to something like 'CONNECTION LOST' / 'BAD TOKEN' (distinguish 403 from other errors), and update engineTxt to reflect the failure. Never leave the status bar showing the optimistic default after a failed poll.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. pollStatus (index.html:603-610) wraps the whole body in try/catch with an empty catch at line 609. The initial status markup (line 60) hard-codes background:var(--acc) (the accent/green dot) and text 'SYSTEM ONLINE'. On a failed /api/status poll (403 bad token or 500), the catch swallows the error and onlineDot/onlineTxt/engineTxt are never touched, so the optimistic default persists. boot() (line 618) only schedules the first poll at t=2s with no immediate call, so if that first poll fails the green 'SYSTEM ONLINE' dot stays forever. Minor nuance for the report: on a SUCCESSFUL idle poll line 605 sets the dot to var(--faint) (grey), so 'SYSTEM ONLINE' text is shown in both healthy-idle and failure states — the dot color is the only signal and it never turns fault-colored on error. Core defect (empty catch + no failure state on the single liveness indicator) holds.

qa_findings.title: Missing/invalid token produces total silent failure with no recovery UI
qa_findings.impact: TOKEN falls back to '' when ?token is absent from the URL (index.html:126). The '/' page has no token check (server.py:147-148), so the SPA always loads — but every subsequent /api call then 403s (server.py:162-163,245-246). This happens on a plain reload if the token got stripped, a bookmark, a shared link, or navigating to the bare host:port. GET-driven views (status poll, sessions, activity) fail silently; the only feedback is a cryptic transient '✕ bad token' toast that fires solely when the user triggers a POST action, and only for 6s. There is no global 'your session token is missing or invalid — reopen EarCrate from the launcher' state, so the app looks loaded-but-inexplicably-empty.
qa_findings.fix: On boot, if TOKEN is empty, show a blocking banner explaining the session must be opened via the launcher URL. Additionally, treat a 403 from api() as a distinct condition (not a generic error) and render a persistent 'session invalid/expired' banner rather than a disappearing toast.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. TOKEN falls back to '' when ?token is absent (index.html:126). _check_token (server.py:147-148) returns True for any non-/api path, so '/' always serves the SPA (server.py:159-160) regardless of token. Every /api GET then 403s (server.py:162-163) and every POST 403s (server.py:245-246) with {"error":"bad token"}. All GET-driven views (status poll:609, sessions:434, crate:289, activity, etc.) swallow that 403 in empty catches, so they render as loaded-but-empty. The only feedback is post()'s transient 6s '✕ bad token' toast, which fires solely on a user-triggered POST. There is no boot-time TOKEN==='' check and no persistent/blocking banner anywhere. Matches the finding exactly.

qa_findings.title: JUDGE button only appears on renders that already PASSED, so failing gates can never be inspected
qa_findings.impact: The JUDGE control is rendered only when playable = s.kind==='render' && s.passed!==false (line 437). A failed/refused render shows 'WHY ▸' (which just navigates to the Crate screen), never JUDGE. Consequently judgeRender only ever runs on renders already known to pass, so it will almost always toast 'all gates pass' and the pass/fail-reason feature the surface exists for is unreachable for exactly the renders you'd want to diagnose.
qa_findings.fix: Show JUDGE for renders regardless of pass state (keep PLAY gated on pass, but expose JUDGE on failures too), or add a dedicated per-render judge affordance in a renders list.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:437 sets playable = s.kind==='render' && (s.passed!==false). Line 442 renders PLAY/JUDGE/OPEN only inside the `playable` ternary branch; the else branch (any refused render, passed===false) renders only 'WHY ▸' which calls go('crate'). There is no JUDGE affordance anywhere for a failed render, so judgeRender can only ever be invoked on renders already flagged as passing. The tester is right that this makes the pass/fail-reason path unreachable for the exact renders you'd want to diagnose. Minor nuance: judge recomputes v1_1_gates fresh, so a render the session marks 'passed' could still surface a fresh gate FAIL — but that only proves the affordance placement is backwards, not that the finding is wrong. High is fair given the feature's stated purpose is diagnosing failures.

qa_findings.title: Ingest module is a dead control — no way to specify a source folder, so /api/ingest always errors
qa_findings.impact: The 'Ingest' card ('Copy from any drive into the managed library') exposes two buttons, 'Dry-run' and 'Ingest now', wired to libAction('/api/ingest',{apply:false}) and {apply:true}. Neither passes a `sources` field, and there is NO source-folder input anywhere on the Library screen (grep confirms the only 'sources' references in index.html are stat labels, none an input). ingest_sources reads `data.get('sources') or []`, finds it empty, and returns {ok:false,error:'no source folders given'} every time. So the entire advertised primary way to feed the library is non-functional: every click just toasts 'ingest: {"ok":false,"error":"no source folders given"}'.
qa_findings.fix: Add a source-folder input (text field and/or /api/browse_dir picker) to the Ingest card and pass its value as `sources:[...]` in the libAction body. Until then the two buttons should be disabled or the card should prompt for a folder first.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:483 wires the Ingest card's two chips to libAction('/api/ingest',{apply:false}) and {apply:true}; neither passes `sources`. Grep confirms there is NO source-folder input on the Library screen — every 'sources' hit in index.html (lines 226,241,244,272,307,313,319,352,359,392,419,489,557) is a stat label, CSS, or the Setup music-folder field, none an input feeding ingest. Backend ingest_sources (ingest.py:128-130) reads `data.get('sources') or []`, finds it empty, and returns {ok:false,error:'no source folders given'} unconditionally, before touching the DB. libAction (index.html:546) then toasts the raw JSON. The entire Ingest card is non-functional. Note: Scan (read-only source folder from Setup) is a separate working feed path, so this is not the ONLY way to load audio, but the advertised Ingest module is genuinely dead. High confirmed.

qa_findings.title: "Previous" transport button skips FORWARD, not back
qa_findings.impact: The left/back-pointing arrow in the transport (path 'M15 1 L6 6 L15 11', clearly a 'previous' glyph) has onclick="endlessSkip()" and title="Next" — identical to the right/next arrow on line 96. endlessSkip() only ever calls endlessNext(), which does ENDLESS.i=(ENDLESS.i+1)%len. So pressing 'previous' advances to the NEXT track. There is no way to go back a track at all, and the control's direction is a lie.
qa_findings.fix: Add an endlessPrev() that steps ENDLESS.i backward (ENDLESS.i=(ENDLESS.i-1+len)%len) then setPlayer, and wire the left arrow (line 100) to it; fix its title to 'Previous'.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:100 renders a left-pointing glyph: rect at x=1 (bar on left) and path 'M15 1 L6 6 L15 11' (apex at x=6, base at x=15) = an unambiguous previous/⏮ triangle. Yet onclick="endlessSkip()" and title="Next", identical to the right/next arrow at line 96 (rect x=13, path 'M1 1 L10 6 L1 11' pointing right). endlessSkip() (598) only ever calls endlessNext() (597), which does ENDLESS.i=(ENDLESS.i+1)%len. So the back arrow steps forward, the title lies, and no endlessPrev exists anywhere. Confirmed. I'd keep high given the directional glyph is objectively wrong, though note it only has any effect while Endless mode is on.

qa_findings.title: Playlist track count ALWAYS shows "0 tracks", even on success
qa_findings.impact: Backend returns entries as an integer count: `return {"ok": True, "manifest": manifest, "entries": len(entries)}`. The frontend computes `nT=(j.entries&&j.entries.length)||j.count||j.tracks||0`. Calling `.length` on a Number is `undefined`, and `j.count`/`j.tracks` are never returned, so nT collapses to 0 for every response. Both the plOut panel and the toast permanently read "0 tracks" regardless of how many tracks were actually selected — the feature looks completely broken to the user even when it worked.
qa_findings.fix: Either treat entries as a number in the frontend (`const nT = (typeof j.entries==='number'? j.entries : (j.entries&&j.entries.length)) || 0;`) or have the backend also return a numeric `count`. Given entries is already an int, just use `j.entries` directly.
qa_findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED. app.py:2274 returns entries as an int: `{"ok": True, "manifest": manifest, "entries": len(entries)}`. index.html:538 computes `nT=(j.entries&&j.entries.length)||j.count||j.tracks||0`. For a positive int like 5: `5 && (5).length` -> `5 && undefined` -> undefined; `j.count`/`j.tracks` are never returned by the backend; so nT falls through to 0. For 0 entries it is also 0. Result: plOut panel and the toast permanently read "0 tracks" for every response. Fix as proposed (use j.entries directly, it is already an int).

qa_findings.title: "open ▸" playlist link never appears and no playlist file is ever written
qa_findings.impact: The frontend only renders the 'open ▸' folder link when `j.path` exists, but propose_playlist returns `{ok, manifest, entries}` where `manifest` is a JSON manifest-file path string (from write_manifest) — there is no `path` key, so the link never shows. Worse, propose_playlist only *writes a proposal manifest* describing a `create_playlist` op; nothing ever executes it, so no .m3u8/playlist file is actually produced. The button is labeled "Build playlist" and toasts as if a playlist was built, but the user gets no openable output at all.
qa_findings.fix: Return the manifest path (or executed playlist path) under a `path` key the UI reads, and either execute the create_playlist op or relabel the control as 'Propose playlist' so it does not promise a built file.
qa_findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED both parts. (a) index.html:538 renders the open link only when `j.path` is truthy, but propose_playlist returns keys ok/manifest/entries (app.py:2274) with no `path` key — link never shows. Note the manifest JSON path IS returned under `j.manifest` (write_manifest returns a str path, app.py:3681-3688) but the frontend reads `j.path`, so even that is never surfaced. (b) propose_playlist only calls write_manifest to record a *proposal* describing a create_playlist op; it never executes it. execute_create_playlist (app.py:4384) which actually writes the .m3u8 (returns {path, entries}) is only invoked during manifest execution (app.py:4276-4277), which this endpoint never triggers. So the 'Build playlist' button produces no openable playlist file. Misleading label/toast confirmed.

qa_findings.title: Preflight button almost always reports "READY to render" — frontend reads j.failures, backend returns j.warnings/j.ready
qa_findings.impact: The Workbench Preflight gate is misleading on the common path. preflight() computes ok = j.ok!==false && !(j.failures && j.failures.length). But core.preflight() always sets audit["ok"]=True (app.py:3459) for any non-empty pool, and crate_readiness_audit NEVER returns a `failures` key — it returns `warnings` (readiness.py:147) and a boolean `ready` (readiness.py:138). So j.failures is always undefined, the second clause is always true, and ok collapses to j.ok!==false, which is true whenever the pool is non-empty. Result: for a populated-but-not-ready crate (no beds, no foreground, too few sources — exactly the states crate_readiness_audit flags in `warnings`), the toast says "Preflight: READY to render". The frontend never looks at j.ready or j.warnings, so the real veto is silently dropped. Only a completely empty pool (ok:False) shows NOT READY, and even then it can't surface a reason (it looks for j.failures[0]/j.error, but the payload carries `warnings`), so it falls back to the generic 'see crate'.
qa_findings.fix: Read the fields the backend actually returns: ok = j.ok!==false && j.ready===true; and surface j.warnings[0] (not j.failures[0]) as the reason. Alternatively, make core.preflight() also emit a `failures` alias and reflect `ready` into `ok`. Either way the frontend must consult `ready`/`warnings`.
qa_findings.verify_note: Fully verified. Frontend index.html:390 computes `ok = j.ok!==false && !(j.failures && j.failures.length)`. Backend server.py:325-326 routes /api/preflight -> core.preflight(data). preflight (app.py:3448-3462) calls crate_readiness_audit and unconditionally sets audit['ok']=True at line 3459 for any non-empty pool. crate_readiness_audit's return dict (readiness.py:134-150) contains NO `failures` key — it returns `warnings` (line 147) and boolean `ready` (line 138). So j.failures is always undefined -> `!(undefined && ...)` == true -> ok collapses to `j.ok!==false`, which is true for any non-empty pool. A populated-but-not-ready crate (ready:false, warnings non-empty) still toasts 'READY to render'. Empty pool returns {ok:False, ready:False, warnings:[...]} -> ok=false, but the reason lookup `(j.failures||[])[0]||j.error||'see crate'` never reads `warnings`, so it always falls back to 'see crate'. The frontend never consults j.ready or j.warnings. Every clause of the tester's claim is provable.

qa_findings.title: Deep-clean and Identify produce NO visible output — results are computed then discarded
qa_findings.where: server.py:315-318 (run_background) vs app.py:3830-3840 / 3981-3984 return payloads; UI libAction index.html:546, renderActivity/renderStatus index.html:455-469
qa_findings.impact: deep_clean_scan returns a rich assessment (junk list, junk_count, real_songs, empty_folders, art_only_folders, images) and identify_tracks returns proposals/identified/unmatched. But run_background discards fn's return value and responds only {ok:true, started:true}. The frontend then does go('activity'), and the Activity view (renderActivity + renderStatus) renders only st.message / progress / last_error / perf stages — it never displays a junk list or identify proposals. The entire point of Deep-clean (find silence/static/corrupt files, empty/art-only folders) and Identify (propose corrected tags) is invisible in the UI. libAction just toasts 'deepclean/scan: ok'.
qa_findings.fix: Return the assessment synchronously (don't background a read-only scan), or persist results and add an Activity/results panel that fetches and renders junk/proposals. deep_clean writes nothing to disk; identify writes identify_proposals.json but nothing in the UI reads it.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. deep_clean_scan returns a rich assessment (junk/junk_count/real_songs/empty_folders/art_only_folders, app.py:3830-3840) and identify_tracks returns proposals/identified/unmatched (3981-3984), but run_background discards fn's return value and responds {ok:true,started:true} (5229). libAction (index.html:546) then does go('activity') for /identify and /deepclean, and Activity (renderActivity 455-461 + renderStatus 462-469) renders only st.message/progress/last_error plus perf stages — it never renders a junk list or proposals. deep_clean writes nothing to disk; identify writes agent_root/identify_proposals.json (3978) but grep of index.html shows nothing reads identify_proposals/proposals. The assessment is invisible in the UI.

qa_findings.title: Identify failure (missing AcoustID key, etc.) is swallowed; UI falsely reports 'ok'
qa_findings.where: app.py:3951-3954 (identify_tracks returns {ok:false, error:'AcoustID API key required'}) via run_background server.py:315; libAction toast index.html:546
qa_findings.impact: identify_tracks returns {ok:false, error:...} for the common case of no API key (the feature is opt-in/off by default, so this is the DEFAULT first-click experience). That dict is returned from inside the background thread and discarded by run_background (which only catches raised exceptions, not returned error dicts). The HTTP response is {ok:true, started:true}, so libAction's toast reads 'identify: ok' (j.ok !== false) and navigates to Activity — where nothing appears. The user is told the network identify succeeded when it actually did nothing.
qa_findings.fix: Surface backend {ok:false,error} to the client. Since identify's key-check is instant, run it synchronously (or validate the key before backgrounding) and let libAction show the error string it already handles.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed and it is the DEFAULT first-click case (feature off by default). identify_tracks returns {ok:false,error:'AcoustID API key required...'} (app.py:3951-3954) as a RETURNED dict inside the background thread; run_background only catches raised exceptions (5225), not returned error dicts, so the HTTP body is {ok:true,started:true}. libAction's toast (index.html:546) computes (j.ok!==false?'ok':...) -> 'ok' and then go('activity') where nothing appears. The user is told identify succeeded when it did nothing.

qa_findings.title: No UI path to apply or roll back identities — Identify is a dead end
qa_findings.where: index.html:486 (Identify card wires only libAction('/api/identify',{})); routes /api/identify/apply, /api/identify/rollback exist server.py:311-314 with backends app.py:3993, 4091
qa_findings.impact: The Identify card exposes a single action, 'Identify (opt-in)', which only runs the dry-run proposal. There is no button anywhere that calls /api/identify/apply or /api/identify/rollback (grep for 'identify/apply' / 'identify/rollback' in index.html returns nothing). apply_identities has a full simulate→signature→execute→journal flow and rollback_identities exists, but they are unreachable from the UI. A user who identifies tracks can never apply the corrected tags nor undo them. The card's own note ('feed these into reorganize/retag') is not wired either — reorganize derives identity from existing tags, not from identify proposals.
qa_findings.fix: Add Preview-apply / Apply / Rollback controls for identities (mirroring the reorg Preview→Apply→Rollback triad, including passing the signature apply_identities requires).
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. The Identify card exposes exactly one action: libAction('/api/identify',{}) (index.html:486). grep of index.html for identify/apply, identify/rollback, apply_identities and proposals returns only line 486 — no button calls /api/identify/apply or /api/identify/rollback. Those routes exist (server.py:311-314) with full backends apply_identities (app.py:3993, simulate->signature->execute->journal) and rollback (rollback_identities). apply_identities also REQUIRES an approved signature (4053-4057) which no UI ever supplies. Identities can be proposed but never applied or undone from the UI.

qa_findings.title: Partial-failure reorganize is reported as 'refused' and loses the rollback journal
qa_findings.where: index.html:520 (reorgApply: if(j.ok){...journal...} else toast(...'apply refused')); backend app.py:442 returns {ok: not errors, moved, journal}
qa_findings.impact: reorganize_source returns ok=(not errors): if ANY file move fails, ok is false even though other files WERE moved and a journal WAS written (moved>0, journal set). reorgApply only stores REORG.journal and reports success inside the if(j.ok) branch; on ok:false it toasts 'apply refused (re-preview)' and never captures j.journal. So after a partial-failure reorg, files have actually been relocated in place, the UI tells the user it was 'refused' (implying nothing happened), and REORG.journal is never set — leaving the user with a half-moved library and NO reachable Rollback for the moves that did occur.
qa_findings.fix: Treat a response containing a journal/moved>0 as an applied (possibly partial) reorg: always store j.journal when present, and report moved count plus any errors rather than a flat 'refused'.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. reorganize_source's apply path returns {ok: not errors, moved, errors, journal} (librarian/ingest.py:442): any single failed move makes ok=False even though other files were moved (moved>0) and a real journal path was written (423-436). reorgApply (index.html:520) only stores REORG.journal and reports success inside if(j.ok); on ok:false it toasts j.error||'apply refused (re-preview)' and never captures j.journal. Extra evidence the tester didn't flag: the apply-error response returns key 'errors' (plural array), NOT 'error', so j.error is undefined and the toast ALWAYS reads 'apply refused (re-preview)'. Result: files were relocated, journal exists on disk, but REORG.journal stays null and Rollback is unreachable while the UI implies nothing happened.

qa_findings.title: Perf 'Stages this run' panel is permanently empty — reads wrong response shape
qa_findings.impact: renderPerfStages does `const p=await api('/api/perf'); const stages=(p&&(p.stages||p.steps))||[];`. But last_perf() returns `{ok, path, ledger:{...}}` and the stage array lives at `ledger.stages` (built in _perf_new_ledger app.py:335, filled by _perf_stage app.py:370-386). Neither `p.stages` nor `p.steps` ever exists at the top level, so `stages` is always [] and the panel always shows 'no completed run yet — book a set' even after many completed runs. The entire Perf timing feature is dead. Should read `p.ledger && (p.ledger.stages)`.
qa_findings.fix: Change to `const L=(p&&p.ledger)||{}; const stages=L.stages||L.steps||[];`. The per-stage fields the code then reads (s.name, s.seconds) do exist on ledger stages (app.py:373,384), so only the top-level access is wrong.
qa_findings.verify_note: server.py:170 returns core.last_perf(), which (app.py:433-438) yields {ok, path, ledger}. The stage array is at ledger.stages (created app.py:335, appended app.py:385). index.html:473 reads `p.stages||p.steps` at the TOP level — neither exists there — so stages is always [] and the panel always renders the 'no completed run yet' empty state (line 474), even after successful runs. The per-stage fields it then reads (s.name line 372, s.seconds line 384) DO exist on ledger stages, so the tester is right that only the top-level access is broken and the fix `p.ledger.stages` is correct. Feature is effectively dead.

qa_findings.title: Re-saving the workspace nests it one level deeper each time, orphaning the DB, analysis cache, manifests and judgments
qa_findings.where: index.html:552,560,580 -> server.py:262 -> app.py:761 configure_workspace / app.py:596 derive_workspace_paths
qa_findings.impact: This is the config-resolution trap. First save works: configure_workspace(music, workspace) derives working_root=workspace/'work', agent_root=workspace/'agent', etc., and configure() persists them (as_dict has no field for the parent 'workspace' folder). But renderSetup re-populates the 'Workspace' input from cfg.working_root (`const ws=cfg.working_root||d.workspace_folder`, line 552) — i.e. the .../work SUBDIR, not the folder the user picked. On any return to Setup (or after toggleWorkers, which calls renderSetup) the field shows .../work. Clicking Save again sends workspace_folder=.../work, so derive makes working_root=.../work/work and agent_root=.../work/agent. Path-separation check passes (still outside music), so it succeeds SILENTLY and creates a fresh empty workspace one level deeper. The previous agent_root (SQLite DB, .npz analysis cache, manifests, loop/atom human judgments) is orphaned and the app now points at an empty tree. Every extra Save re-nests: work -> work/work -> work/work/work. The field label 'Workspace - all output lives here' actively misleads, since it is bound to a subdir.
qa_findings.fix: Persist and expose the workspace ROOT the user chose (add a workspace_root/base field to Config.as_dict and default_paths), and populate setWs from that, not from working_root. Alternatively, if workspace_folder already ends in /'work' (or already contains agent/ + work/), treat it as an existing workspace root instead of re-deriving under it. At minimum, make configure_workspace idempotent so saving the current config twice is a no-op.
qa_findings.verify_note: Fully confirmed from code. Config.as_dict() (earcrate/core/config.py:21-33) persists master_root/working_root/agent_root/etc. but has NO field for the parent workspace folder the user picked. default_paths returns configured=as_dict() (app.py:556). renderSetup binds the Workspace input from working_root: `const ws=cfg.working_root||d.workspace_folder||''` (index.html:552), i.e. the .../work SUBDIR. saveWorkspace posts that value as workspace_folder (index.html:580). configure_workspace -> derive_workspace_paths sets working_root=workspace/'work', agent_root=workspace/'agent' (app.py:601-602), so a second save yields .../work/work and .../work/agent. The separation check in configure() only compares master vs working and master vs agent (app.py:522), never working vs its prior location, so it passes and the new tree is created silently while the old agent_root (SQLite DB, .npz cache, manifests, judgments) is orphaned. configure_workspace (app.py:761-773) is not idempotent. The 'Workspace - all output lives here' label (index.html:559) is bound to the subdir. Also note toggleWorkers calls renderSetup() (index.html:581), re-triggering the repopulation. Real high-severity data-orphaning bug.

qa_findings.title: Render ▸ ignores the proposed/saved/loaded plan and recomposes from scratch
qa_findings.where: index.html:429 renderMix(); server.py:301 /api/one_click_bg; app.py:3300 one_click_taste_mix -> 3401 propose_taste_mashup -> 3407 execute
qa_findings.impact: The Workbench presents a pipeline 'Propose plan -> inspect three rails -> Save plan -> Render ▸', strongly implying Render renders the plan you are looking at. It does not. renderMix() posts /api/one_click_bg with only {taste_profile:S.resident}; one_click_taste_mix recomposes a brand-new arrangement via propose_taste_mashup(params) with a fresh next_render_seed and renders that. window.__plan is never sent, and there is NO endpoint anywhere that renders a saved/loaded plan. Consequence: the seed/BPM/rails/source picks the user reviewed, saved, and loaded are thrown away at render time; saved plans are effectively view-only and can never be reproduced as audio. This is the core promise of the surface being broken.
qa_findings.fix: Pass window.__plan (or its plan_hash) to the render path and have one_click_taste_mix accept a supplied arrangement/seed to render deterministically instead of always recomposing. At minimum, wire loadPlan/propose to feed the exact seed into /api/one_click_bg so the rendered output matches the previewed plan.
qa_findings.verify_note: renderMix() at index.html:429 posts /api/one_click_bg with only {taste_profile:S.resident}. server.py:302 routes it to run_background(one_click_mix,data); one_click_mix (app.py:2349) delegates to one_click_taste_mix (3300), which composes a brand-new arrangement via propose_taste_mashup (3010). Since data carries no seed, propose_taste_mashup line 3019-3020 takes explicit_seed=False and draws a fresh next_render_seed(c.seed). window.__plan is never sent in the body, and grepping every route confirms only /api/timeline/{propose,save,load,list} exist — none render a stored plan. So the seed/BPM/rails the user reviewed, saved, and loaded are discarded at render time and saved plans cannot be reproduced as audio. The tester's cite of 'one_click_taste_mix' is one indirection off (route calls one_click_mix, a one-line passthrough) but the defect is exactly as described. Keeping high: the surface still produces audio, but its central promise (render the plan you inspected) is broken.

qa_findings.title: Activity screen never surfaces the finished render — the whole payoff of "Book a set" is invisible where the user is dropped
qa_findings.where: index.html:283/429 go('activity') → renderStatus index.html:462-470; backend sets status.last_render_path app.py:3418
qa_findings.impact: bookSet and renderMix both navigate to Activity to "watch". renderStatus renders only message, progress %, and last_error (grep confirms renderActivity reads no other status field). On success one_click sets status['last_render_path'] and a "complete" message, but Activity offers no way to play/open the render and no link to Sessions. The user watches the run finish and then sees an idle screen with no playable result; they must independently discover that the mix lives under Sessions. The core deliverable is produced but not shown at the point of completion.
qa_findings.fix: When status.last_render_path is present and busy is false, render a PLAY/OPEN affordance (reuse setPlayer/openFolder) or a "View in Sessions" button in renderStatus.

qa_findings.title: Structured refusal detail is discarded in background mode — user only gets a single error string
qa_findings.where: run_background target app.py:5222-5224 discards fn() return; one_click refusals build rich dicts at app.py:3363 / 3372 / 3427 via _finish_one_click_result
qa_findings.impact: When a booked set refuses (readiness short, library exhausted, or post-render rejection), one_click_taste_mix returns a detailed dict — per-role shortfalls (readiness.failures/have/need), yield projections from _taste_harvest_projection, and the full harvest_log — via _finish_one_click_result. But because it runs through run_background, target() calls fn and ignores its return value entirely. Only the single set_status(msg,...) string reaches the UI (Activity last_error). All the "what to feed the crate" diagnostics the code paid to compute are thrown away; the user just sees one long sentence like "TasteSpec crate refused theater: ...".
qa_findings.fix: Persist the finish result onto status (e.g. status['last_result']) so Activity/Sessions can render the harvest log, per-role need/have, and yield projections, not just the flattened message.
qa_findings.verify_note: run_background.target() (app.py:5222-5228) calls fn(*args,**kwargs) and ignores the return value; the rich dicts built by _finish_one_click_result at app.py:3363/3372/3421/3427 (per-role readiness have/need, _taste_harvest_projection yields, full harvest_log) never reach the polled status. Only set_status(msg,...) is surfaced, and Activity renders just that string. One nuance the tester omitted: _finish_one_click_result does persist the full result to disk via _run_bundle_finish (app.py:309), so it is not gone forever -- but nothing on this surface reads the run bundle, so the UI-facing claim holds. Confirmed medium.

qa_findings.title: "Book a set" button ignores the readiness verdict shown right next to it
qa_findings.where: index.html:249 (always-enabled glowing button) vs verdictOf index.html:192-196 and readiness_pct display index.html:220,200-201
qa_findings.impact: A resident can render as "WANTS MATERIAL" (r.ready=false, red verdict) with a low readiness_pct, yet the Book-a-set button is always enabled, glowing, and gives no pre-flight warning or confirm. Clicking it launches the full doctor→scan→harvest pipeline that (for a nonzero-but-short pool) can churn a long time and then refuse. The UI already knows the resident isn't ready (r.ready is present in the same payload) but does not gate, warn, or annotate the button. (This is acceptable and even documented for the pool_size==0 / never_auditioned case via the r.wants copy, but not for the short-pool case.)
qa_findings.fix: When activeResident().ready is false and pool_size>0, either disable the button with an inline "crate short — will likely refuse" note, or show a confirm that names the missing roles from r.wants before firing bookSet.
qa_findings.verify_note: The Book-a-set button (index.html:249) is hardcoded with animation:glowPulse and no disabled attribute or onclick guard -- it is always live regardless of activeResident().ready. verdictOf (index.html:192-196) returns the red "WANTS MATERIAL" verdict when r.ready is false, and readiness_pct is displayed (index.html:200-201,220), so the UI already holds the readiness signal but bookSet (index.html:280-284) fires unconditionally with no pre-flight warn/confirm. Confirmed medium.

qa_findings.title: migrate/plan response is discarded — user sees no count, actions, or human summary
qa_findings.impact: libAction's toast reads only j.dry_run, j.planned, and j.ok. The plan response has no 'planned' field; it returns summary, actions (the full preview list), and a rich 'human' description string. So the toast collapses to "plan: DRY-RUN — ok" regardless of how many items would migrate/quarantine/scrub. The user gets no visibility into what the migration would actually do — the entire preview payload (its whole reason for existing) is thrown away.
qa_findings.fix: Render the plan's human/summary/actions in the Health & migration card (e.g. into doctorOut or a dedicated panel) instead of only firing libAction's generic toast.
qa_findings.verify_note: Verified. libAction toast (index.html:546) builds its message from only j.dry_run, j.planned, and j.ok. The plan response (app.py:911-915) has NO 'planned' field, so `j.planned!=null` is false and it falls through to the `j.ok!==false ? 'ok'` branch; with dry_run:true and ok:true the toast collapses to 'plan: DRY-RUN — ok' regardless of scale. The rich payload — summary (per-op counts + bytes), actions (full preview list, app.py:902-903), and the human sentence (app.py:907-910) describing migrate/quarantine/scrub counts — is never rendered anywhere. Preview payload is thrown away. Severity medium as stated.

qa_findings.title: Pairs panel shows the SELECTED atom as its own pairing partner for every edge where it sits on the right side
qa_findings.impact: compatible_pairs_for_atom fetches edges WHERE e.left_atom_id=? OR e.right_atom_id=?, but the SELECT only returns the RIGHT atom's identity (rt.artist right_artist, rt.title right_title, ra.ear_role right_role) — it never returns the left atom's artist/title. renderCratePairs always renders p.right_artist/p.right_title as 'the other atom'. The graph is built with left in {foreground,bass,spark} and right in {beds, foreground}, so a bed atom (or any atom on an edge's right side) appears as the right_atom of its own edges. When such an atom is selected, every pair row displays that same atom's name as the partner instead of the vocal/bass/spark it actually pairs with. The relation label (e.g. 'vocal_over_bed') and score are correct, but the named partner is wrong/self-referential. Beds are a whole role category, so the panel is meaningless for them.
qa_findings.fix: Have compatible_pairs_for_atom also select the LEFT atom identity (la joins -> left_artist,left_title,left_path) and, in renderCratePairs, pick the side that is NOT the selected atom_id (compare p.left_atom_id/p.right_atom_id to a.atom_id) before rendering the partner name.
qa_findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED. compatible_pairs_for_atom (app.py:5142-5161) SELECTs only right-atom identity (rf.path right_path, rt.artist right_artist, rt.title right_title) plus la.ear_role left_role / ra.ear_role right_role — the LEFT atom's artist/title/path are never returned. The WHERE clause is `(e.left_atom_id=? OR e.right_atom_id=?)`, so the selected atom can be the RIGHT side of an edge. renderCratePairs (index.html:343) unconditionally renders `esc((p.right_artist||'')+' '+(p.right_title||p.right_role||''))`, i.e. always the right side. build_compatibility_graph (app.py:2887-2898) builds edges as vocal_over_bed(left=foreground,right=beds), bass_over_drums(left=basses,right=beds), spark_into_phrase(left=sparks,right=beds+foreground[:40]) — so BED_CHORD/DRUM_BREAK/TEXTURE atoms only ever appear on the right side, meaning a selected bed shows its own name as the partner on every row. Foreground atoms are mixed (correct partner on vocal_over_bed edges, self on spark edges where they land on the right). Severity medium is fair: relation label and score are correct, only the named partner is wrong, and it's total nonsense specifically for the whole beds role category.

qa_findings.title: Favorite / Lock / Reject buttons give a toast but never refresh the crate, so they appear to do nothing
qa_findings.where: index.html:349-353 judgeAtom; index.html:308-316 renderCrateList; ear/readiness.py:258-271 rank_material output
qa_findings.impact: judgeAtom posts to /api/ear_atoms/judgment and shows an optimistic toast, but unlike judgePair (which calls renderCratePairs() after) it never re-renders the list or inspector. The crate list is populated from /api/rank -> rank_material, whose per-atom output dict contains no favorite/locked field and only status='approved' atoms are in the pool at all. Consequences: (a) after Reject the atom stays visible in the ranked list until the whole Crate screen is manually re-opened/re-ranked, so the user sees no change and may re-click; (b) Favorite and Lock produce NO persistent visual state anywhere in the crate (no star/lock badge is rendered and the data to render one isn't even fetched). All three atom-judgment controls read as no-ops even though the backend persisted the judgment.
qa_findings.fix: After a successful judgeAtom, re-fetch/re-render (call renderCrate or at least drop rejected atoms from DATA.ranked and re-render). Include favorite/locked in rank_material's output and render a badge in renderCrateList so Favorite/Lock have a visible effect.
qa_findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED. judgeAtom (index.html:349-353) only awaits the POST and calls toast() — no re-render, in contrast to judgePair (index.html:354) which calls renderCratePairs() after. set_atom_judgment (app.py:5123) does persist ear_atoms.status='rejected', but the crate list comes from /api/rank -> rank_crate -> approved_atom_pool, which filters `a.status='approved'` (app.py:2771), so a rejected atom only disappears on a manual re-rank/re-open of the Crate screen — it stays visible after Reject. rank_material's per-atom output dict (readiness.py:258-271) contains no favorite/locked/status field, and renderCrateList (index.html:308-316) renders no star/lock badge, so Favorite and Lock produce zero visible state anywhere. All three controls read as no-ops even though the backend persisted the judgment. Severity medium is fair.

qa_findings.title: Global QUERY box is a no-op on 6 of the 7 screens
qa_findings.impact: The QUERY box lives in the always-visible command strip (with placeholder 'bpm:104-118 role:vox'), implying app-wide search. But onQuery() only does anything when S.mode==='crate' (it calls renderCrateList, which reads el('query').value at index.html:303). On Play, Library, Workbench, Sessions, Activity, and Setup, typing in the box filters nothing and gives no feedback — a prominent global control that is dead on the majority of screens. There is also no visual cue that the box is crate-only, and the typed query is neither cleared nor surfaced when navigating.
qa_findings.fix: Either scope the QUERY box to the Crate screen (hide/disable it elsewhere, or move it into the crate surface), or make onQuery() dispatch to the active screen's list renderer. At minimum, grey/disable the input when S.mode!=='crate' and update the placeholder to say it filters the crate.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. index.html:585 onQuery() = `if(S.mode==='crate') renderCrateList();` — the only handler wired to the always-visible command-strip input (index.html:43). el('query').value is read solely in renderCrateList (index.html:303); grep shows no other consumer. On Play/Library/Workbench/Sessions/Activity/Setup, typing filters nothing and gives no feedback, and the box is never disabled/greyed or its placeholder changed off-crate. Note the separate Workbench playlist input is #plQuery (line 509), distinct from this global #query. Medium is fair.

qa_findings.title: First-boot lands on Play with red 'WANTS MATERIAL' verdicts and no path to Setup
qa_findings.where: index.html:614 boot -> go('play') -> renderPlay (index.html:207); verdictOf index.html:192; app.py:5030
qa_findings.impact: On a fresh install self.config is None. boot() routes straight to Play, which fetches /api/residents. Because taste_readiness raises 'not configured' per profile, each resident entry comes back with only an 'error' field set (app.py:5031) — no 'ready', no 'never_auditioned'. verdictOf() then falls through to the default branch and paints every resident 'WANTS MATERIAL' in fault red (index.html:196), while updateChrome shows crate-fit '–'. A first-time user sees a fully-chromed, alarming Play screen and is never directed to Setup (nav item #06) to configure a workspace first. The underlying 'not configured yet' error is swallowed and never surfaced.
qa_findings.fix: Detect the unconfigured state on boot (e.g. GET /api/defaults returns configured:null at app.py:556) and route first-run users to Setup, or render an explicit 'configure a workspace to begin' empty state on Play instead of red per-resident verdicts derived from a swallowed config error.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed as a deterministic code cascade for the unconfigured state. boot() (index.html:614-621) has no ec_mode persistence, so S.mode defaults to 'play' and go('play')->renderPlay runs. renderPlay calls /api/residents, which routes straight to core.residents() with no ensure_config guard (server.py:186-187). In residents() each profile calls taste_readiness->approved_atom_pool->conn()->connect_db()->ensure_config(), which raises RuntimeError('earcrate is not configured yet') when self.config is None (app.py:970-972). That exception is swallowed into entry['error'] (app.py:5030-5031), so entries carry NO ready/never_auditioned/readiness_pct. verdictOf (index.html:192-197) then falls through to the red 'WANTS MATERIAL' default, and updateChrome shows crate-fit '–' (index.html:200). No routing to Setup on unconfigured boot. Depends only on config being None on a fresh install, which is the documented default of ensure_config.

qa_findings.title: Audio playback failures (403/404/416 from /api/audio) are completely silent
qa_findings.impact: Audio never goes through api(); it is set as `<audio>.src = audioUrl(...)` and started with `a.play().catch(()=>{})` (index.html:589,590). The /api/audio route can return 403 (path outside workspace/library, server.py:224), 404 (file missing, server.py:227), or 416 (bad range, server.py:120). All of these leave the audio element in an error state, and the empty `.catch(()=>{})` swallows the play rejection. The user clicks ▶ / Book a set / Play demo and nothing happens, with zero feedback. Note the token IS correctly attached to the audio URL, so this is purely an error-visibility gap, not an auth gap.
qa_findings.fix: Attach an 'error' listener to the <audio> element (and inspect a.error) to toast something like 'Audio unavailable (missing file or outside library)'. Replace the empty play().catch with one that surfaces the failure.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. Audio bypasses api(): playClip (index.html:589) and setPlayer (index.html:590) set a.src=audioUrl(...) and call a.play().catch(()=>{}) — empty rejection handler. togglePlay (591), endlessNext, playDemo all funnel through these. The /api/audio route can return 403 (path outside workspace/library, server.py:224), 404 (missing file, server.py:227), or 416 (bad range, server.py:120), all leaving the <audio> element in an error state. boot() (index.html:616) attaches only 'ended', 'play', and 'pause' listeners — there is NO 'error' listener and no inspection of a.error anywhere. The token IS correctly attached (audioUrl, line 158, appends &token=), so this is purely an error-visibility gap. User clicks ▶ and gets zero feedback. Confirmed.

qa_findings.title: Error responses render as indistinguishable 'empty' states, masking failures as no-data
qa_findings.impact: Several GET/POST-backed views catch the error and then fall through to render an 'empty' fallback identical to a legitimately-empty result. Example: renderCrate does `let ranked=[]; try{...}catch(e){}` (index.html:289) then renders 'crate empty — build the ear crate in Library first' (index.html:316) — so a 403/500 on /api/rank is presented to the user as an empty crate, prompting them to redo work that already exists. renderSessions does the same: catch(e){} (index.html:434) then 'no sessions yet' (index.html:444). The user is told the wrong thing (data is missing) when the real problem is the request failed.
qa_findings.fix: Distinguish thrown errors from genuinely-empty results: on catch, render an explicit error state (with e.message / e.trace) instead of the neutral empty-state copy, so a failure never masquerades as 'nothing here yet'.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed for both cited views. renderCrate: `let ranked=[]; try{...post('/api/rank'...)...}catch(e){}` (index.html:289), and renderCrateList falls through to '|| crate empty — build the ear crate in Library first' (index.html:316) — a 403/500 on /api/rank is presented as an empty crate, telling the user to rebuild work that may already exist. renderSessions: `let items=[]; try{...api('/api/sessions')...}catch(e){}` (index.html:434) then '|| no sessions yet — book a set from Play' (index.html:444). In both, a failed request is indistinguishable from a genuinely empty result. The pattern (empty accumulator + empty catch + neutral empty-state copy) is exactly as described.

qa_findings.title: Reference comparison harness is unreachable — UI hardcodes ref:'' so judge_audio_file never runs the ref branch
qa_findings.impact: judgeRender always posts {path, ref:''}. server.py:338 coerces it via str(data.get('ref') or '') or None => None. judge_audio_file therefore always takes ref_path=None and never populates out['reference'] / out['delta_vs_reference'] (audio.py:235-244). The Addendum A0 reference-comparison harness has no UI entry point at all; there is no control to pick a reference file, so 'judging against a ref' cannot happen from the app.
qa_findings.fix: Add a reference picker (or drag-target) that passes a real ref path, and render the returned delta_vs_reference; otherwise the ref parameter and its backend branch are dead.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:450 posts {path, ref:''} with a literal empty string. server.py:338 coerces via str(data.get('ref') or '') or None, which collapses '' to None. audio.py:168/235 only enters the reference branch `if ref_path:`, so out['reference'] and out['delta_vs_reference'] (audio.py:236-244) are never populated from the UI. Grepped the whole frontend surface: there is no ref picker, drag target, or any control that would pass a non-empty ref. The backend ref branch and delta_vs_reference are dead code from the app's perspective. Medium is fair.

qa_findings.title: Failing-gate 'reason' shown to the user is just raw metric keys with no threshold or direction
qa_findings.impact: On failure the toast reads e.g. 'FAIL — rms_std_db, silence_ratio, low200_share'. These are internal metric names; the user gets no measured value, no threshold, and no direction (e.g. rms_std_db must be >=4.5, low200_share >=0.48). The TasteSpec pass/fail 'reason' is technically surfaced but not human-readable, and it is truncated to the first 3 via slice(0,3) with no indication more failed.
qa_findings.fix: Map each gate key to a plain-language sentence with the measured value vs threshold (data is in j.render), and indicate when more than 3 gates failed.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:450 builds `failed` from Object.entries(g).filter(v===false).map(([k])=>k) and toasts 'FAIL — '+failed.slice(0,3).join(', '). g comes from v1_1_gates, whose values are pure booleans (audio.py:227-232), so only internal keys like rms_std_db/silence_ratio/low200_share/distinct_pcs are shown — no measured value, no threshold, no direction. The actual numbers exist in the response as j.render (audio.py:212-224) but are never used. slice(0,3) truncates without any 'and N more' indicator; note there are only 4 gates total so truncation is at most one hidden key. Confirmed; medium/low is fair.

qa_findings.title: judge_render can 500 on a missing/undecodable render; user sees the raw exception string (and trace is leaked in the response)
qa_findings.impact: judge_render passes the path straight to judge_audio_file with no existence/format guard; decode_audio (audio.py:172) will raise if the file was deleted after listing or is an unsupported/corrupt format. server.py returns 500 with {'error': str(exc), 'trace': traceback.format_exc()}. post() (index.html:157) surfaces j.error, so the user gets a raw Python exception message instead of a friendly 'render not found / could not decode', and the full traceback is shipped to the client. judgeRender's own empty catch{} adds nothing since post already toasted.
qa_findings.fix: In judge_render, validate the path is inside the renders root and exists before decoding, and return a structured error; stop returning the traceback field to the browser.
qa_findings.verify_note: app.py:5213-5215 judge_render passes render_path straight into judge_audio_file with no existence/inside-root/format guard. audio.py:172 calls decode_audio, which (analyze/decode.py:27-31) raises RuntimeError on ffmpeg non-zero return (missing/corrupt/unsupported file) or zero decoded samples. server.py:337-338 has no try/guard around the judge call, so it hits the generic except at 340-348 returning HTTP 500 {'error': str(exc), 'trace': traceback.format_exc()}. api() (index.html:155) throws new Error(j.error) which post() (157) toasts, so the user gets a raw Python/ffmpeg exception string; the full traceback is shipped to the browser in the response body. judgeRender's empty catch{} adds nothing. Confirmed; medium.

qa_findings.title: Loop-review 'Lock' chip is a mislabeled duplicate of 'Approve', and 'Approve' is always shown highlighted regardless of the loop's real status
qa_findings.impact: loopRow renders three chips: chip('Approve','approved',true), chip('Reject','rejected',false), chip('Lock','approved',false). The 'Lock' chip fires the exact same call as 'Approve' — loopSetStatus(id,'approved') → POST /api/loop_status status='approved' — so 'Lock' does nothing distinct from Approve (set_loop_status already locks on approve). Separately, the Approve chip's highlighted state is hardcoded on=true, so EVERY candidate loop shows Approve lit up as if already approved, giving no visual signal of the loop's actual status.
qa_findings.fix: Either remove the redundant 'Lock' chip or point it at a real lock action; drive the chip highlight from l.status (on = (l.status===status)) instead of hardcoded booleans.
qa_findings.verify_note: loopRow (index.html:524,531) renders chip('Approve','approved',true), chip('Reject','rejected',false), chip('Lock','approved',false). Both Approve and Lock call loopSetStatus(id,'approved') → POST /api/loop_status (index.html:535). The route (server.py:276) calls set_loop_status(loop_id, status) with no locked arg, so locked defaults to True (app.py:2188) on any approve — meaning Lock produces the identical DB effect as Approve and is redundant. Separately the Approve chip's `on` flag is hardcoded true, so the highlight is static: every candidate loop renders Approve lit up regardless of its real l.status, giving no visual signal of actual state. Both halves confirmed. Medium confirmed.

qa_findings.title: Library screen shows a raw backend exception string when the app is unconfigured, with no path back to Setup
qa_findings.where: loadTracks (index.html:544) and loadLoopReview (index.html:534); backend list_tracks/list_loops → conn() → connect_db() → ensure_config() (app.py:970-972)
qa_findings.impact: If Setup was never completed, /api/tracks and /api/loops throw 'earcrate is not configured yet' (500). The frontend catch blocks render esc(e.message) in red directly into the Tracks and Loop-review panels. The user sees the internal error text rather than a 'configure a workspace in Setup first' prompt. The nice empty-state ('no tracks — Scan a music folder in Setup') only fires on a successful-but-empty response, never on the unconfigured error.
qa_findings.fix: Detect the unconfigured/500 case in loadTracks/loadLoopReview and render a friendly 'Set up your workspace in Setup' call-to-action instead of the raw exception message.
qa_findings.verify_note: Traced the full chain: GET /api/tracks → list_tracks (app.py:2143) → conn() (1014) → connect_db() (996) → ensure_config() raises RuntimeError('earcrate is not configured yet') (app.py:971-972). Server catches at server.py:233-236 and returns _json(500,{'error':str(exc)}). Frontend api() does `throw new Error(j.error||txt)` (index.html:155), so e.message IS the raw exception. loadTracks catch (index.html:544) renders esc(e.message) in var(--fault) red directly into the panel; loadLoopReview catch (index.html:534) does the same into #loopList plus 'loops unavailable'. The friendly empty-state ('no tracks — Scan a music folder in Setup', line 543) only appears on a successful-but-empty response, never on the 500. Confirmed. Medium confirmed.

qa_findings.title: Per-loop status chips show a hardcoded state, not the loop's real status
qa_findings.impact: loopRow renders the three chips with a literal on/off flag: chip('Approve','approved',true) + chip('Reject','rejected',false) + chip('Lock','approved',false). The third arg controls the highlighted (accent-background) styling, so EVERY row always renders 'Approve' as highlighted/active and 'Reject'/'Lock' as inactive, regardless of l.status. Because the list is fetched with ?status=candidate, every visible row is an un-approved candidate, yet each one displays a green 'already approved' badge. The toggle never reflects persisted state, so a reviewer cannot tell approved from rejected from candidate by looking at the chips — the visual state is meaningless.
qa_findings.fix: Drive the third arg from data, e.g. chip('Approve','approved', l.status==='approved'), chip('Reject','rejected', l.status==='rejected'). Since the list only pulls candidates, either also surface approved/rejected rows or drop the fake 'active' styling entirely.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:524 chip=(label,status,on)=> uses `on` only to decide accent styling (var(--accink)/var(--acc) vs dim). Line 531 hardcodes it: chip('Approve','approved',true)+chip('Reject','rejected',false)+chip('Lock','approved',false). The `on` flag is a literal, never derived from l.status, so every row renders 'Approve' highlighted and the others inactive regardless of the loop's actual status. Since loadLoopReview (534) fetches ?status=candidate, every visible row is an un-approved candidate yet shows a green 'approved' badge. The chip highlight carries no real information. Confirmed defect; medium is right (misleading state on the primary review control, but data-mutating clicks still work).

qa_findings.title: Candidate list silently truncated to 100 with no pagination or 'showing X of Y'
qa_findings.impact: loadLoopReview renders only items.slice(0,100). list_loops fetches up to limit=1000 (default), and the /api/loops route never passes a limit, so the backend can return up to 1000 rows but the UI shows at most the top-100 by score and drops the rest with no indicator. The counts header still shows the true candidate total (e.g. 'candidates 500'), so a user sees 500 candidates but only 100 rows and no page controls. Candidates ranked #101+ cannot be individually reviewed/approved/rejected at all — only bulk quota-approve or reject-all can touch them, which defeats the 'human review' purpose for large pools.
qa_findings.fix: Add pagination or at least a 'showing 100 of N — refine or use quota' notice; wire an offset/limit through /api/loops (list_loops already accepts limit) or lazy-load more rows.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:534 renders items.slice(0,100). server.py:180 calls list_loops(status) passing only status, so limit defaults to 1000 (app.py:2157). The counts block (2173-2186) is computed by a separate un-limited GROUP BY, so the header shows the true candidate total while at most 100 rows render. Candidates ranked #101-1000 by score are unreachable for individual approve/reject — only quota-approve or reject-all can touch them. No pagination, offset, or 'showing 100 of N' notice exists. Confirmed; medium is right for large pools.

qa_findings.title: Transport prev/next arrows are dead outside Endless mode
qa_findings.impact: endlessSkip() is a no-op unless ENDLESS.on is true. When the user is auditioning a single atom (playClip) or a booked render loaded via setPlayer without endless engaged, both skip arrows do nothing and give no feedback (no toast). The controls look active (class="clk") but silently do nothing on the most common playback path.
qa_findings.fix: Either disable/grey the arrows when !ENDLESS.on, or make them fall back to seeking / a toast explaining Endless must be on.
qa_findings.verify_note: endlessSkip() at index.html:598 is `if(ENDLESS.on) endlessNext();` — a pure no-op when ENDLESS.on is false. setPlayer() (590) is used to load single atoms/renders without touching ENDLESS.on, and playDemo only sets it on for the demo path. Both arrows carry class="clk" (look interactive) but do nothing and emit no toast on the single-audition path. Confirmed, medium.

qa_findings.title: STATION "CUT" button does not cut or skip the playing track
qa_findings.impact: The 'CUT' control calls station('skip'), which only POSTs to /api/station/feedback; the backend returns applies:'logged only' and does nothing to playback (no queue advance, no pause). The label 'CUT' and the ⏭ toast imply the current track is dropped/skipped, but audio keeps playing unchanged. Users will expect it to behave like a radio skip.
qa_findings.fix: Have the skip signal also call endlessNext() (when endless is on) after the feedback POST, or relabel/clarify that CUT only logs a taste signal.
qa_findings.verify_note: index.html:114 CUT -> station('skip') (600) which only POSTs /api/station/feedback and toasts '⏭ logged'. Backend station_feedback (app.py:5067) for signal=='skip' skips the fire/ice bias branches, only bumps receipts/journals, and returns applies:'logged only' (5090). Nothing pauses or advances the <audio>; station() never calls endlessNext(). Label 'CUT' + ⏭ icon imply a radio skip; audio keeps playing. Confirmed, medium.

qa_findings.title: Endless silently excludes renders that lack a current-engine report
qa_findings.impact: endlessQueue() keeps only items where r.current_engine is truthy. current_engine is engine_version==ENGINE_VERSION, and engine_version is null whenever a render has no sidecar .render_report.json (or an older-engine one). Such renders ARE returned by /api/renders and play fine when loaded individually, but Endless treats them as nonexistent — toggleEndless() shows 'No renders yet' even though renders sit in the folder. A user with real renders whose reports are missing/stale gets a confusing dead Endless button.
qa_findings.fix: Relax the filter (e.g. include renders when current_engine is not explicitly false, mirroring the quality_gate_passed!==false pattern), or surface a distinct message like 'renders exist but are from an older engine'.
qa_findings.verify_note: endlessQueue() (index.html:595) keeps only `r.current_engine && r.quality_gate_passed!==false`. In list_renders (app.py:4142) engine_version is None when the .render_report.json sidecar is missing (4152-4155) or holds an older engine, so current_engine = (engine==ENGINE_VERSION) is False (4164) and the item is dropped. Those same renders ARE returned by /api/renders and load/play fine via setPlayer individually. If all renders lack current reports, toggleEndless (596) hits the empty branch and toasts 'No renders yet' despite renders existing. Note the asymmetry: quality_gate_passed uses a lenient !==false but current_engine uses strict truthiness. Confirmed, medium.

qa_findings.title: Query placeholder advertises a syntax the parser ignores
qa_findings.where: index.html:509 (placeholder "query e.g. bpm:110-124 role:vox") vs app.py:2247-2259
qa_findings.impact: propose_playlist only recognizes: a BPM range matching regex `(\d{2,3})\s*[-–]\s*(\d{2,3})\s*bpm` (i.e. "110-124 bpm", with the word 'bpm' AFTER the range), and the literal keywords instrumental/low vocal/vocal. The advertised example `bpm:110-124` (colon form, 'bpm' before) does NOT match the regex, and `role:vox` is parsed by nothing. A user copying the placeholder gets a silently unfiltered full-library result (ordered only by energy), with no indication the query was ignored.
qa_findings.fix: Make the placeholder match the actual grammar (e.g. "e.g. 110-124 bpm instrumental") or extend the parser to handle bpm:/role: tokens.
qa_findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED. Placeholder at index.html:509 is `query e.g. bpm:110-124 role:vox`. Parser (app.py:2247-2259) lowercases q and only matches BPM via regex `(\d{2,3})\s*[-–]\s*(\d{2,3})\s*bpm` — requires the literal word 'bpm' AFTER the range. On the placeholder string 'bpm:110-124 role:vox' the digits '110-124' are followed by ' role', not 'bpm', so no match. `role:vox` triggers nothing: parser only tests substrings 'instrumental'/'low vocal'/'vocal', and 'vocal' is not a substring of 'role:vox'. A user copying the placeholder gets an unfiltered full-library result ordered by energy, with no signal the query was ignored. Placeholder does not match the real grammar.

qa_findings.title: Preflight audits a different dataset (legacy loops) than Readiness/Graph (taste atoms) and ignores taste_profile
qa_findings.where: app.py:3449 (preflight -> approved_loop_pool()) vs app.py:2816 (taste_readiness -> approved_atom_pool(taste_profile)) and app.py:2878 (build_compatibility_graph -> approved_atom_pool)
qa_findings.impact: The frontend sends {taste_profile:S.resident} to /api/preflight (index.html:390), but core.preflight() never reads data['taste_profile'] and calls self.approved_loop_pool() — which queries the `loops` table (app.py:2437-2446), a legacy per-loop pool with no taste-profile filter. Meanwhile the Readiness panel and Build-pair-graph both audit approved_atom_pool(taste_profile) (ear atoms). So the Workbench Preflight can report READY/NOT-READY based on a pool that has no relationship to the resident taste profile the rest of the Workbench composes against, producing contradictory verdicts vs the Crate Readiness panel.
qa_findings.fix: Make preflight() take and honor taste_profile and audit the same pool the composer uses (approved_atom_pool / the atom-based readiness), or clearly label the Preflight card as auditing the raw loop pool so the two panels aren't confused for each other.
qa_findings.verify_note: Verified. Frontend posts {taste_profile:S.resident, target_seconds:120} to /api/preflight (index.html:390). core.preflight (app.py:3448-3449) sets params from data but reads `pool = self.approved_loop_pool()` — never reads data['taste_profile']. approved_loop_pool (app.py:2437-2451) queries the legacy `loops` table with WHERE l.status='approved' and NO taste_profile filter. By contrast taste_readiness (app.py:2816) and build_compatibility_graph (app.py:2878) both call approved_atom_pool(taste_profile), which queries `ear_atoms` filtered by taste_profile. Server route (server.py:296) passes taste_profile only into taste_readiness, confirming preflight silently discards it. So Preflight can report READY/NOT-READY over a pool unrelated to the resident taste profile the rest of the Workbench composes against.

qa_findings.title: Readiness panel goes stale after judging atoms — approving/rejecting doesn't refresh have/need
qa_findings.impact: DATA.readiness is fetched once when the Crate screen renders (index.html:291) and renderReadiness runs once (299). judgeAtom() approves/rejects an atom (which changes approved_atom_pool and therefore taste_readiness have{}/failures{}) but only toasts — it does not re-fetch /api/taste/readiness or call renderReadiness/renderCrateList. So the whole point of the readiness panel (judge atoms to clear the shortfalls) gives no live feedback; the bars and the ranked list stay frozen until the user navigates away and back to Crate.
qa_findings.fix: After a successful judgeAtom, re-fetch readiness and re-run renderReadiness (and optionally re-rank the list), or invalidate DATA.readiness and re-render.
qa_findings.verify_note: Verified. renderCrate fetches DATA.readiness once (index.html:291) and calls renderReadiness once (line 299). judgeAtom (index.html:349-353) posts /api/ear_atoms/judgment and only toasts on success — it does not re-fetch /api/taste/readiness, and does not call renderReadiness, renderCrateList, or renderCrate. Since a judgment changes approved_atom_pool and therefore taste_readiness have{}/failures{}, the readiness bars and the ranked list stay frozen until the user navigates away and back. Confirmed.

qa_findings.title: reorgApply moves original master files IN PLACE with no confirmation dialog
qa_findings.impact: The Apply button physically shutil.move()s the user's ORIGINAL library files (the card even says 'Move the ORIGINAL files in place'). reorgApply fires the destructive POST with no window.confirm — it relies solely on REORG.signature being set, which the immediately-preceding Preview click auto-satisfies. By contrast reorgRollback (521) and manifest execute (452) both confirm before writing. Apply sits directly beside Preview in the same button row (485), so a mis-click starts an in-place relocation of the entire library with no 'are you sure'.
qa_findings.fix: Add a window.confirm to reorgApply summarizing the pending count (e.g. 'Move N original files in place? Reversible via Rollback.') before POSTing apply.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. reorgApply (index.html:520) POSTs /api/reorganize/apply with no window.confirm; it guards only on REORG.signature, which the adjacent Preview button auto-set. Backend shutil.move()s the originals in place (librarian/ingest.py:434; the tester cited app.py:426-441 but reorganize_source lives in librarian/ingest.py and is bound via app.py:5239 — same code, minor path mislabel). By contrast reorgRollback (521) and execManifest apply (452) both window.confirm first. Apply sits directly beside Preview in the same action row (485).

qa_findings.title: Rollback is only reachable within the same browser session (in-memory journal)
qa_findings.impact: The reorg journal path is held only in the in-memory REORG.journal JS variable. If the user applies a reorganize and then reloads the page (or navigates away and the SPA re-inits), REORG.journal is null and reorgRollback aborts with 'Nothing to undo — no applied reorganize this session', even though the journal file still exists on disk under agent_root/reorg_journal. The most likely moment to want a rollback (come back later, realize the tree is wrong) is exactly when it's unreachable.
qa_findings.fix: Persist/list recent reorg journals server-side and let the UI discover the latest journal for rollback instead of relying solely on session memory.
qa_findings.verify_note: Confirmed. The journal path lives only in the in-memory JS var REORG.journal (index.html:518 init null, 520 set). reorgRollback (521) aborts with 'Nothing to undo — no applied reorganize this session' when REORG.journal is null, which is the state after any page reload/SPA re-init. The journal file itself persists on disk at agent_root/reorg_journal/reorg-*.jsonl (librarian/ingest.py:423), and grep confirms server.py exposes only reorganize/plan, /apply, /rollback — no route lists or discovers existing reorg journals, so the UI has no way to recover the path for a later rollback.

qa_findings.title: Perf stages never refresh after a run completes while Activity is open
qa_findings.impact: renderPerfStages() is only invoked when pollStatus is called with force=true, which happens once on entering Activity (renderActivity -> pollStatus(true)). The 2s background loop calls pollStatus(false) (boot line 618), so it updates the live status card but never re-fetches /api/perf. If a compile finishes while the user is watching Activity, the stage ledger for that run does not appear until they navigate away and back. (Compounded by the shape bug above, it never appears at all.)
qa_findings.fix: Call renderPerfStages() when a run transitions busy->idle (e.g. track previous st.busy and refresh on the falling edge), or refresh perf on each poll while in activity mode.
qa_findings.verify_note: renderPerfStages() is called only inside `if(force)` in pollStatus (index.html:608). renderActivity passes force=true once (line 460), but the 2s background loop calls pollStatus(false) (line 618), which updates renderStatus but never re-fetches /api/perf. So a run finishing while the user watches Activity does not surface its stage ledger until they re-navigate. Confirmed as a real missing-refresh defect (independent of, and compounded by, Finding 1).
